plantings.variety: Super Star + Yellow Sweet Spanish (~18 each)

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Pre-fruit feed — bone meal
tasks.description: Side-dress 1 tbsp bone meal per plant once first flowers appear. Supports fruit set and meets phosphorus demand during flowering.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Mid-season feed — blood meal
tasks.description: Work 1 tbsp blood meal per plant into top inch of soil. Apply once heavy fruiting begins to compensate for nitrogen demand. Skip if foliage is already very dark green — excess nitrogen pushes leaf growth at expense of fruit.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: First harvest check
tasks.description: Roma and paste types: firm and fully colored. Slicers: slight give when squeezed. Do not wait for perfect color in heat — fully vine-ripened tomatoes go soft fast in summer temperatures.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Harvest check
tasks.description: Harvest when lower 3-4 leaves have browned but upper 5-6 are still green. Do not wait until all leaves are brown — the wrapper will deteriorate. Dig carefully with a fork, do not pull by the stem.

tasks.title: Harvest check
tasks.description: Harvest when lower 3-4 leaves have browned but upper 5-6 are still green. Do not wait until all leaves are brown — the wrapper will deteriorate. Dig carefully with a fork, do not pull by the stem.

tasks.title: Fall renovation
tasks.description: After last harvest in fall, cut all foliage back to 1 inch above crown. Remove old mulch and debris. Add 1 inch of fresh compost to the bed. Apply fall Berry-Tone feeding at label rate. This resets the plant for next year.

tasks.title: Early spring feed — Berry-Tone
tasks.description: Apply True Organic Berry-Tone at label rate (approximately 1/2 cup per 5 sq ft of bed) as soon as new growth appears in early spring. Work lightly into top inch of soil and water in well.

logs.note: Bajillion flowers

Watering hard for one hour. Also added more nutrients in the form of berry food and worm castings to try and help.
Final yield only 1 lb. Delicious. I think I needed to plant a little earlier and water more generously.
Looking really rough. Added fertilizer and watered full hose for 1 hour to try and kinda bring it back but she is a sad little tree this year.
4 sprouted. 2 knocked back by a late frost but I think they will come back
Sprouts! Sprouted today already a couple inches tall. Six of the 8 so far.
Pods just starting to appear

forecast.detailedForecast: Patchy smoke. Sunny, with a high near 96. West southwest wind around 7 mph.

forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Patchy smoke before midnight. Mostly clear. Low around 67, with temperatures rising to around 73 overnight. East wind 3 to 10 mph.

forecast.name: Wednesday
forecast.shortForecast: Mostly Sunny then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast.detailedForecast: A slight chance of rain showers between noon and 3pm, then a slight chance of showers and thunderstorms. Mostly sunny. High near 97, with temperatures falling to around 93 in the afternoon. South wind 8 to 15 mph. Chance of precipitation is 20%.

forecast.detailedForecast: A chance of showers and thunderstorms before midnight. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 64. South southeast wind 8 to 13 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%.

forecast.name: Thursday Night

payload.headline: Garlic off zone 2 now — 24 days to harvest.
weather_notes: Do all garden work before 9 AM Monday through Wednesday — heat climbs from 92°F to 97°F with no rain in the 7-day forecast.
Re-run zone 1 by Wednesday morning — today's run will be 3 days old at Wednesday's 97°F peak, which may stress 24-day bean seedlings in c2–c4 and the potato bags.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today — zone 2 ran this morning but w2 and w3 must be excluded from all future zone 2 runs now; both garlic beds are 24 days from harvest and the dry-down cannot wait another day.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest ripe blackberries before heat peaks
suggested_tasks.description: Berries first observed June 14 — 7 days on the vine plus 95–97°F heat arriving Tuesday will over-ripen and soften remaining fruit within 1–2 days.

suggested_tasks.title: Check e1 Amish Paste for suckers
suggested_tasks.description: At 43 days with no active sucker task for this planting, indeterminate Amish Paste is in peak lateral growth — 97°F heat by Wednesday will push suckers past the 2-inch pinch window.

payload.headline: Garlic cutoff today — 97°F heat wave arrives mid-week.
weather_notes: Inspect e1 tomatoes for hornworms and blight before 9 AM Mon–Wed — 92°F Monday escalates to 97°F Wednesday, peaking both pest feeding and disease risk.
Check potato_bags moisture daily this week — grow bags dry significantly faster than ground beds during the 95-97°F mid-week stretch with zero rain forecast.
Mulch exposed soil on c2–c4 bean beds before Tuesday — 24-day-old seedlings face their first 95°F day with no rain in the forecast.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today — zone 2 ran this morning on the deadline; remove w2 from all future zone 2 cycles with harvest 24 days out.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Overdue by 1 day on 43-day Amish Paste — 95-97°F mid-week heat drives peak hornworm feeding; check lower leaves for frass this morning.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest ripe blackberries before heat spoils them
suggested_tasks.description: Berries first observed Jun 14 — 7 days of 89-97°F heat means fruit is at or past peak and will soften fast in this week's 95°F+ stretch.

payload.headline: Stop w2 garlic water now — deadline is tomorrow.
weather_notes: Complete all garden tasks before 10 AM — Fire Weather Watch active today until 10 PM MDT brings gusty wind and extreme low humidity.
Check potato_bags soil moisture today — grow bags dry out faster than ground beds under Fire Weather Watch conditions and bags last watered June 18.
Run zone 1 Sunday morning if tonight's isolated storms (30% chance) deliver nothing — tomatoes and beans last watered June 18 face 88–91°F heat through Monday.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow — zone 2 last ran June 18 and harvest is 25 days out; exclude w2 from all future zone 2 runs starting today.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today on 42-day Amish Paste — 7 days of 89–97°F heat drives peak hornworm feeding; check lower leaves for frass this morning.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest ripe blackberries before they soften
suggested_tasks.description: Berries first observed June 14 — 6 days of 89–97°F heat will have ripened and begun softening fruit; pick now before they pass peak.

payload.headline: Stop w2 garlic water — 2 days to June 21 deadline.
weather_notes: Do all garden work before 9 AM today — Heat Advisory continues through Saturday morning and 97°F midday conditions make outdoor work unsafe.
Add or check mulch depth on e1–e4 tomato beds — 97°F soil surface loses moisture fast and 2-3 inches of mulch extends time between zone 1 runs.
Run zone 1 as scheduled Saturday morning — 2 days dry and 90°F forecast warrants it; pause only if afternoon showers arrive before the scheduled run.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: June 21 deadline is 2 days away with harvest 26 days out — yesterday's zone 2 run may have been the last acceptable irrigation before the dry-down window must begin.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow and today's 97°F drives peak hornworm feeding — check lower leaf undersides for frass this morning before the task goes overdue.

surfaced_tasks.title: Mid-season feed — blood meal
surfaced_tasks.reason: 41 days from transplant with first harvest 31 days out — the heavy fruiting window is opening now, and Sunday's cooler 88°F after possible Saturday rain is the ideal application window.

payload.headline: 97°F Friday — blight check overdue, cut garlic water.
weather_notes: Remove w2 and w3 garlic from zone 2 irrigation starting now — today's run should be their last with the Jun 21 stop-water deadline 3 days away and harvest 27 days out.
Do all tomato scouting and plant work before 9 AM Friday — 97°F Juneteenth heat peaks by midday with no wind relief forecast.
Monitor Saturday afternoon's slim storm chance (12%) — if showers arrive, skip the next zone 1 run to avoid waterlogging 21-day bean seedlings in c2–c4.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due yesterday on 40-day Amish Paste — inspect lower leaves this morning before 97°F Friday intensifies stress on heat-loaded foliage.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due in 2 days — hornworm feeding accelerates with 97°F arriving Friday; check and treat before peak heat conditions.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Deadline is 3 days away with harvest 27 days out — today's zone 2 run should be w2's last before the Jun 21 cutoff.

suggested_tasks.title: Train new blackberry canes to trellis
suggested_tasks.description: Five new canes observed Jun 14 — tie to trellis structure while still pliable before 97°F Friday hardens new wood.

payload.headline: 97°F arrives Friday — water now, blight check tomorrow.
weather_notes: Water e1–e4, c1–c4, and potato_bags today — zone 1 last ran June 14 and heat climbs from 93°F today to 97°F Friday with zero rain forecast.
Water w1, w4, and blackberry today — zone 2 also last ran June 14 and the same dry heat window applies through the weekend.
Exclude w2 and w3 garlic from all remaining irrigation — 29 days to July 15 opens the 2-week dry-down window right now.
Check c2–c4 bean seedling soil daily this week — 19-day sprouts in 93-97°F with no rain can dry out at the surface between zone 1 runs.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow on 38-day Amish Paste — inspect lower leaves now before 93–97°F heat through Friday accelerates any latent infection.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due in 5 days with harvest 29 days out — the 2-week dry-down must begin today to protect wrapper quality.

suggested_tasks.title: Train new blackberry canes to trellis
suggested_tasks.description: Five new canes observed June 14 — tie them to the trellis while growth is still flexible before Friday's 97°F hardens new wood.

suggested_tasks.title: Check w3 Italian Red for scapes
suggested_tasks.description: Hardneck Italian Red at 29 days from harvest — snap any curled scapes now to redirect energy into bulbs before the dry-down window closes.

payload.headline: 92°F arrives Tuesday — pull w2 garlic off irrigation now.
weather_notes: Water e1–e4 tomatoes, c1–c4 beans, and potato_bags by Tuesday — 2 days since the Jun 14 irrigation with 92°F heat arriving and no rain through the week.
Exclude w2 garlic from Tuesday's watering — the Jun 21 stop-water deadline is 6 days away with harvest 30 days out.
Water w1, w4, and blackberry no later than Wednesday — 88–92°F heat all week will stress these beds 3+ days from the Jun 14 zone 2 run.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: The Jun 21 cutoff is 6 days away with harvest ~30 days out — this is the week to permanently remove w2 from irrigation scheduling.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due Wednesday on 37-day tomatoes — inspect lower leaves before 88–92°F heat peaks Tuesday through Thursday.

suggested_tasks.title: Train new blackberry canes to trellis
suggested_tasks.description: Five new canes observed Jun 14 — tie to the trellis structure while growth is still flexible before 92°F heat hardens new wood.

suggested_tasks.title: Check w3 Italian Red for scapes
suggested_tasks.description: 30 days to harvest — snap any curled scapes now to redirect energy into bulbs, last window before the harvest dry-down.

payload.headline: Pick strawberries, water the peach — 93°F by Tuesday.
weather_notes: Hand-water the peach tree today — it's been dry since Jun 3 and 93°F arrives by Tuesday.
Skip extra watering on w1, w2, w3, w4, and blackberry today — zone 2 already ran this morning.
Pick ripe w1 strawberries today — 87-93°F heat through Tuesday will soften and spoil fruit left on the plant.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check peach tree for signs of recovery
surfaced_tasks.reason: Now 3 days overdue and dry since Jun 3, with highs climbing to 93°F by Tuesday — check before the heat forecloses any recovery window.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today, and three straight days of 87-93°F heat will push lateral growth past the 2-inch pinch window.

suggested_tasks.title: Hand-water the peach tree today
suggested_tasks.description: 11 days since its last watering (Jun 3) and 93°F arrives by Tuesday — water now regardless of recovery odds to limit further stress.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest ripe strawberries before heat spoils them
suggested_tasks.description: Seascape and Flamingo have been actively fruiting since early June, and 87-93°F through Tuesday will soften any ripe berries fast.

payload.headline: 93°F Tuesday — check e1 tomatoes, peach, harvest strawberries.
weather_notes: Skip watering today — both Rachio zones already ran this morning, well ahead of Tuesday's 93°F peak.
Harvest ripe strawberries and tomatoes promptly — 90-93°F heat through Tuesday spoils and softens fruit fast.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today, and the climb to 93°F by Tuesday accelerates lateral growth past the 2-inch pinch window.

surfaced_tasks.title: First harvest check
surfaced_tasks.reason: 9 days overdue, and 90-93°F heat through Tuesday will soften any ripe fruit fast.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check peach tree for signs of recovery
surfaced_tasks.reason: 3 days overdue on a tree last logged as likely dead — confirm status before Tuesday's 93°F adds further stress.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest ripe strawberries before heat spoils them
suggested_tasks.description: Pick ripe w1 berries today — 90-93°F heat through Tuesday spoils fruit fast left on the vine.

payload.headline: Heat returns — water everything except garlic, recheck peach.
weather_notes: Water e1–e4, c1–c4, and potato_bags today — zone 1 has sat dry since Wednesday, with no rain through Monday's 89°F.
Water w1, w4, and blackberry today too, but skip w2/w3 — zone 2 is also 3 days dry, and garlic benefits from drying out ahead of the Jun 21 cutoff.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check peach tree for signs of recovery
surfaced_tasks.reason: 2 days overdue, and today's mild dry 86°F is the easiest check-in before highs climb toward 89°F by Monday.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow — today's 86°F kicks off a hot, dry run to 89°F by Monday that will push lateral growth past the 2-inch pinch window.

suggested_tasks.title: Set up trellis support for c1 beans
suggested_tasks.description: Set up poles or trellis for c1 Good Mother Stollard now — at 41 days with no support, Monday's 89°F surge will accelerate vine sprawl.

payload.headline: Peach and c2 checks overdue — 86°F heat returns today.
weather_notes: Deep-water the peach tree today — 9 days since its last watering, with 86°F heat resuming after the cool snap.
Water c2–c4 bean beds and potato_bags by Sunday — zone 1's cycle is 2 days old and 86–88°F, rain-free days run through Monday.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check peach tree for signs of recovery
surfaced_tasks.reason: Now 1 day overdue and 9 days dry — confirm whether new growth justifies keeping the tree or planning a replacement.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check c2 gaps, replant quail-damaged spots
surfaced_tasks.reason: Now 1 day overdue at day 15 since planting — quail-damaged gaps need seed before the sprouting window closes.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due Sunday — today's return to 86–88°F heat will accelerate lateral growth on indeterminate Amish Paste before suckers exceed 2 inches.

suggested_tasks.title: Check c1 runner beans for trellis support
suggested_tasks.description: At 40 days with no support logged, set up poles or trellis for c1 Good Mother Stollard before this weekend's 86–88°F surge speeds up vine growth.

payload.headline: Hornworm check due today; replant c2 gaps before Friday's heat.
weather_notes: Replant c2 quail-damage gaps tomorrow — use the cool 77°F window before Friday's 88°F heat arrives.
Scout e1–e4 and bean beds for pests and heat stress today or tomorrow — 77–81°F beats Friday's 88°F for inspection comfort.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today, and today's mild 81°F is the easiest inspection window before Friday's 88°F heat returns.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check c2 gaps, replant quail-damaged spots
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow — quail-damaged gaps from Jun 4 need seeds now while the sprouting window is still open.

suggested_tasks.title: Check peach for new growth, deep-water
suggested_tasks.description: Last log (Jun 3) feared the tree was dead with no follow-up since — check for new growth and deep-water before Friday's 88°F heat.

payload.headline: Cool today and Thursday — scout pests, replant c2, check peach.
weather_notes: Skip watering through Thursday — both Rachio zones ran today and 77-81°F won't stress soil before Friday's 88°F heat.
Inspect e1 for hornworms today — mild 77-81°F is the easiest scouting window before Friday's heat returns.
Check the peach tree for new growth before Friday's 88°F — confirm whether last week's emergency watering worked.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check c2 gaps, replant quail-damaged spots
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow, and today's mild 77-81°F gives new seeds time to imbibe before Friday's 88°F heat returns.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today, and 32-day-old Amish Paste is in peak hornworm season with mild conditions ideal for spotting frass.

suggested_tasks.title: Check peach tree for signs of recovery
suggested_tasks.description: 7 days since the Jun 3 'one more watering' — assess new growth now while it's mild, before Friday's heat and before it's too late to plan a replacement.

payload.headline: Hornworm check due tomorrow; 87°F returns Friday.
weather_notes: Water e1–e4, c1–c4, and potato_bags by Thursday — beds last irrigated Jun 8 will be 5 days dry when Friday's 87°F arrives.
Skip w2/w3 garlic zones in Thursday's watering — 87°F Friday accelerates bulb curing with the Jun 21 stop-irrigation deadline 12 days out.
Use today's 77°F window for pest scouting — best inspection conditions before Wednesday warms and Friday's 87°F returns.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow — 31-day Amish Paste tomatoes are in peak hornworm window; today's 77°F is ideal for checking lower leaves for frass.

surfaced_tasks.title: Stop watering
surfaced_tasks.reason: Garlic harvest is 36 days out and the Jun 21 cutoff is 12 days away — exclude w3 from Rachio scheduling before Friday's 87°F advances curing further.

suggested_tasks.title: Check c2 gaps, replant quail-damaged spots
suggested_tasks.description: Day 12 since planting and the sprouting window closes this week — bare spots from the Jun 4 quail disturbance won't self-fill and need seeds now.
